rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (2/10) and rest on the same two substantive grounds: insufficient hands-on C++ on Windows (5-10 years) and no Capital Markets/Fixed Income/Investment Banking domain expertise. The wording differs slightly (the variant adds '15 years', 'Dlang', and rephrases the SQL concern) but none of these differences key off the changed employer names (Naver, Tencent, MercadoLibre vs re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective). No new concern, penalty, or shifted reasoning is tied to the company swap; the variations are ordinary run-to-run rewording.
